input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows App SDK / WinUI3 requires the Windows SDK and MSVC tooling:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want the build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required components:

```powershell
# Modify the VS installation to include MAUI + UWP/WinUI components
# Run from an elevated prompt:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --includeRecommended --quiet --wait
```

## Step 4 — Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload bundles an Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and verify paths:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    # Fallback: check for standalone Android SDK
    $androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Android\Sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK at: $androidSdk"

# List installed platforms & build-tools dynamically
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

If you need a specific API level:

```powershell
# Discover available platform versions
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install a specific one (e.g., API 35)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35"
```

## Step 5 — Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Persist ANDROID_HOME so builds can find the SDK
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")

# Also add platform-tools to PATH for adb access
$currentPath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("PATH", "User")
$platformTools = Join-Path $androidSdk "platform-tools"
if ($currentPath -notlike "*$platformTools*") {
    [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("PATH",
        "$currentPath;$platformTools", "User")
}
```

## Step 6 — Android Emulator (Optional)

```powershell
# Install emulator + a system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
    --device "pixel_6"

# Ensure Windows Hypervisor Platform is enabled (needs reboot)
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online `
    -FeatureName "HypervisorPlatform" -NoRestart
```

## Step 7 — Enable Developer Mode (Windows Target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading WinUI3 / MSIX apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 8 — Create & Validate a Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Deploy to Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -p:AndroidAttachDebugger=false
```

## Step 9 — Discover the Right TFM Dynamically

If you're unsure which `net9.0-windowsX.X.X` TFM to use:

```powershell
# List installed Windows SDK versions
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Platforms\UAP" |
    Select-Object Name

# List target frameworks the template supports
dotnet new maui --dry-run -n Probe 2>&1 |
    Select-String "TargetFrameworks"

# Or inspect the generated .csproj
(Select-Xml -Path .\HelloMaui.csproj `
    -XPath "//TargetFrameworks").Node.InnerText -split ";"
```

---

**Key version-pinning tip:** Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to keep Android SDK bindings and Windows App SDK in sync with your .NET SDK version.
